author: bridge-bot
content:
<leofanxiong> Cysic’s zkVM, Venus, is open-sourced.

- Release announcement: https://x.com/cysic_xyz/status/2039753797967421917?s=46

- Code repo: https://github.com/cysic-labs/venus (https://github.com/cysic-labs/venus)

author: kevaundray
content:
very cool! I had two questions:

- What is the performance difference between Venus and ZisK? 
- Does the computational graph approach get better with higher gas limits?

author: bridge-bot
content:
<leofanxiong> re: **@L1zkevm_bot**
> <kevaundray> very cool! I had two questions:

- What is the performance difference between Venus and ZisK? 
- Does the computational graph approach get better with higher gas limits?
- this pr last week shows a 6% speedup: https://github.com/0xPolygonHermez/pil2-proofman/pull/452
We keep improving the performance.

- yes, observed better performance for larger blocks.

FYI, here’s the soundness cal pr: https://github.com/ethereum/soundcalc/pull/79
